turns.content: What brings you in today?
It's my knees, doc. They've been acting up something fierce for the past few months. Mornings are the worst - I can hardly get out of bed without wincing.
When did this start?
Probably around six months now, maybe a little more. It's been so gradual I can't say exactly when it started, but I know it's been getting worse as the weather's turned.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's a dull ache deep in both knees, right where the joint is. Sometimes it gets sharper when I try to straighten them out or climb stairs. Mostly it's just a constant, nagging soreness that never really goes away.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No fever, no chills. Just the plain joint pain. I've never run hot or anything like that with it.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take ibuprofen when the pain gets real bad, and sometimes acetaminophen too. I try not to take them too often, but some days I need both to get through. I can't take aspirin, though - I'm allergic.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
Well, I've got rheumatoid arthritis, been dealing with that for years now. And I'm overweight too, which I know doesn't help my knees none. That's about it, doctor.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Yes, aspirin. I found that out the hard way a few years back. Broke out in hives and felt real sick. So I stay far away from anything with aspirin in it.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've been using that heating pad on my knees at night, and I try to rest them when they're real bad. Sometimes I'll skip stairs if I can help it and take the elevator instead. But nothing really takes it away completely.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Cold weather makes it worse, for sure. And sitting too long without moving - that's when I really stiffen up. Heat helps, like with my heating pad or a warm bath. But to be honest, nothing makes it go away completely, just takes the edge off.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It makes getting around harder than it used to be. I can't walk to the grocery store like I used to, and standing at the counter for more than a few minutes leaves me hurting. Some days I just feel tired and worn down from dealing with it all morning.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
Never smoked a day in my life, and I don't drink alcohol either. Just never had the taste for it.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
My mother had arthritis real bad, especially in her hands and knees. She used to complain about it all the time, just like I do now. So I guess it runs in the family.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

Well, I've had arthritis aches off and on for years, that's nothing new. But this knee pain is worse and more constant than what I'm used to. It used to come and go, but now it's just there all the time.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's a solid 7 or 8 when I first get up in the morning. It settles down some during the day, but never really goes below a 4 or 5. Emotionally, I'm just tired of it, you know? It wears on you, always hurting. But I don't dwell on it much - just get on with things as best I can.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think that pretty much covers it. The knees are the main thing bothering me these days. If you've got any ideas on how to get some relief, I'm all ears.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I just take it one day at a time. Some days are easier than others. I rest when I need to, use the heating pad, and try not to let it get me down too much. Not much else a person can do, is there?
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I just get through it as best I can. I spend a bit more time getting moving in the mornings, wait for the stiffness to pass, and do what I can around the house. I don't complain much - no point in it. Just keep plugging along.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I already told you, I just take it as it comes. I don't let it rule my life, but I won't pretend it's easy either. Is there something else you need to ask me?
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Like I said, I'm managing. I get through the mornings slow, rest when I need to, and don't make a fuss. If you're asking if I'm depressed or something, I'm not - just sore and a bit worn out.
